pairs.question: How many teams suspended operations at the end of the 2006–07 ECHL season, and why?
pairs.answer: Two teams suspended operations: the Long Beach Ice Dogs and the Toledo Storm. Toledo's suspension was requested by Toledo Arena Sports, Inc. to allow time for building a new arena in downtown Toledo, planned to open in 2009.

pairs.question: Which team returned to the ECHL in the 2007–08 season after a two-season suspension and why were they suspended?
pairs.answer: The Mississippi Sea Wolves returned after suspending operations for two seasons (2005–07) due to damage to the Mississippi Coast Coliseum caused by Hurricane Katrina.

pairs.question: What changes occurred to the Trenton, New Jersey franchise before the 2007–08 ECHL season?
pairs.answer: The Trenton franchise, owned by the NHL New Jersey Devils, adopted the Devils nickname and entered its ninth season under the new name.

pairs.question: What new award was introduced by the ECHL in the 2007–08 season and what does it honor?
pairs.answer: The ECHL introduced the Ryan Birmingham Memorial Award to honor on-ice referees for their dedication. It honors Ryan Birmingham, a former ECHL referee who died in a car accident at age 24.

pairs.question: Which team won the Brabham Cup and the Kelly Cup in the 2007–08 ECHL season, and how did they perform in the playoffs?
pairs.answer: The Cincinnati Cyclones won both the Brabham Cup (regular season) and the Kelly Cup (playoffs). They defeated the Las Vegas Wranglers four games to two in the finals, becoming the third team in ECHL history to achieve this double victory in the same season.

pairs.question: What significant event led to the creation of the Ryan Birmingham Memorial Award?
pairs.answer: The award was created to honor Ryan Birmingham, a former ECHL referee who died in May 2007 at age 24 in an automobile accident while traveling from Chattanooga, Tennessee to Snellville, Georgia.

pairs.question: How many teams joined the ECHL in the 2007–08 season and from which league did one of them come?
pairs.answer: One team, the Elmira Jackals, joined the ECHL in the 2007–08 season after previously competing in the United Hockey League.
